Patch Set 2:

I realized that this patch doesn't save/load the fileset info to the nodemap config file, so it would have to be re-set on mount. Maybe this is best done in a separate patch, but it would be nice to have that functionality.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Kit, the ability to store the fileset in the MGS config file will depend on that patch landing.  I think it is OK to add the /proc interface to set this, since it can be saved in an "lctl set_param -P" tunable, if your patch is modified to load the nodemap before the config_param llog.

We also discussed the ability to have a per-nodemap or a per-fileset config log for the clients, so that it would be possible to have different tunables for different clients.

Patch Set 2:

I am gonna push a new version of this patch without the dependency on LU-28, as this dependency was introduced artificially only to make it possible to submit patch at http://review.whamcloud.com/18784 (which truly depends on both 'fileset mount' and 'fileset in nodemap' features).
Not having the dependency on LU-28 may ease landing of this patch.

Patch Set 3:

> Kit, the ability to store the fileset in the MGS config file will
 > depend on that patch landing.  I think it is OK to add the /proc
 > interface to set this, since it can be saved in an "lctl set_param
 > -P" tunable, if your patch is modified to load the nodemap before
 > the config_param llog.
 > 
 > We also discussed the ability to have a per-nodemap or a
 > per-fileset config log for the clients, so that it would be
 > possible to have different tunables for different clients.

Andreas, Kit,


I have to confess I am a little bit lost here, as it is unclear to me what I have to do in order to save fileset information of nodemap in MGS llogs. Following your remark about 'lctl set_param -P', is there any specific thing to do? If yes, could you point me to data structures I should extend and functions I should add?

Moreover, I probably do not have the right code base to do this work. Could you please specify the patches required (already merged or not) to base my work?


Thanks,
Sebastien.

Patch Set 3:

I looked at the MGS code and it should load the nodemap config before the llogs. The nodemaps are loaded in mgs_fs_setup and that is called before llog_setup in mgs_init0. So the set_param -P trick should work for all parameters that affect the MGS.

Patch Set 3:

Here is what happens when I run 'lctl setparam -P':


# lctl set_param -P nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset='myfset1 /mydir1'

# mount -t ldiskfs -o loop /var/loop/mgt1 ./here

# llog_reader here/CONFIGS/params

[...snip...]

Header size : 8192

Time : Fri Mar 25 19:08:49 2016

Number of records: 12

Target uuid : config_uuid

-----------------------

#01 (224)SKIP START marker   2 (flags=0x05, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 19:08:49 2016-Fri Mar 25 19:16:46 2016

#02 (128)SKIP set_param 0:general  1:nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset=krb1 /krb1  2:lctl

#03 (224)SKIP END   marker   2 (flags=0x06, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 19:08:49 2016-Fri Mar 25 19:16:46 2016

#04 (224)SKIP START marker   3 (flags=0x05, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 19:16:46 2016-Fri Mar 25 22:35:33 2016

#05 (128)SKIP set_param 0:general  1:nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset=krb2 /krb2  2:lctl

#06 (224)SKIP END   marker   3 (flags=0x06, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 19:16:46 2016-Fri Mar 25 22:35:33 2016

#07 (224)SKIP START marker   4 (flags=0x05, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 22:35:33 2016-Fri Mar 25 22:41:29 2016

#08 (128)SKIP set_param 0:general  1:nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset=krb1 /krb1  2:lctl

#09 (224)SKIP END   marker   4 (flags=0x06, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 22:35:33 2016-Fri Mar 25 22:41:29 2016

#10 (224)marker   5 (flags=0x01, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 22:41:29 2016-

#11 (128)set_param 0:general  1:nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset= myfset1 /mydir1  2:lctl

#12 (224)END   marker   5 (flags=0x02, v2.7.66.0) general         'nodemap.set_nodemap_fileset' Fri Mar 25 22:41:29 2016-


First, the nodemap fileset info is stored into params llog. It is expected?

Second, we can see that nodemap_fileset command for one nodemap is overwritten by a subsequent command for another nodemap (SKIPs).

So it seems it is going wrong :(

Patch Set 3:

Hi Sebastian, I think that the fileset parameter is a per-nodemap parameter, so you'd have to do something like lctl set_param -P nodemap.<nodemap_name>.set_nodemap_fileset='fileset'. I'll try testing as well.

Patch Set 3:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 3:

> Hi Sebastian, I think that the fileset parameter is a per-nodemap
 > parameter, so you'd have to do something like lctl set_param -P
 > nodemap.<nodemap_name>.set_nodemap_fileset='fileset'. I'll try
 > testing as well.

You are right, using the following syntax prevents previous fileset information from being overwritten:

# lctl set_param -P nodemap.<nodemap name>.set_nodemap_fileset='<nodemap> <fileset>'

But in that case, I guess the syntax of set_nodemap_fileset's value should be adapted, because at the moment it is mandatory to specify nodemap name in there. So it is a duplicate.

Not sure how to retrieve nodemap name as specified in parameter name though.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

OK, now by issuing the following 'set_param -P' command on the MGS the fileset info is stored permanently:

# lctl set_param -P nodemap.<nodemap>.fileset='<fileset>'

Of course, in order to work it also needs patch at http://review.whamcloud.com/11830 to transfer nodemap info from MGS.



The only remaining thing that bothers me now is that fileset info in nodemap is not visible by doing 'cat /proc/fs/lustre/nodemap/<nodemap>/fileset' on MGS node. It is only set on MDS and OSS side when params llog is read.



Is that an issue? Does it mean that 'lctl nodemap_set_fileset --name <nodemap> --fileset /<fileset>' command needs to be run on MGS in addition to 'lctl set_param -P'?

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)
Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

Patch Set 4:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 4:

I have added new tests for fileset in nodemap, in sanity-sec.sh This is done in patch http://review.whamcloud.com/19895.

I have also updated the commit message to explain that the fileset info of nodemaps is stored permanently for servers via the params llog, but not for the MGS (which does not prevent fileset info to be taken into account by servers).
Indeed, MGS does not read params llog upon start. Should it?

Patch Set 13:

> Sebastien, if you are depending on "lctl set_param -P" for this
 > functionality to work, please take a look at LU-7004 and LU-7183 as
 > there have been problems reported with "set_param -P" that may

Hopefully the fileset info into the nodemaps is used by Lustre servers, not clients. So 'set_param -P' works in that case because servers will read 'params' llog.

parents.subject: LU-7064 obd: detect errors from llog_declare_destroy()

commit.subject: LU-7846 nodemap: add fileset info to nodemap
commit.message:
LU-7846 nodemap: add fileset info to nodemap

Give the ability to set a fileset information on a nodemap.
When set, a client pertaining to this nodemap will be allowed to
mount only the subdirectory represented by this fileset.

Fileset info of nodemap is stored permanently by using
'lctl set_param -P' commands. It means MDS and OSS will be able to
retrieve fileset info of nodemap after a restart (when they read
params llog).
MGS will not see fileset info after restart, but this does not prevent
the feature from working.

Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Kit Westneat <kit.westneat@gmail.com>
